Sourdough Discard Sandwich Bread
This sourdough discard sandwich bread is soft and reliable like a sandwich bread should be, but with the added tanginess of sourdough discard. It is quick to make and uses up sourdough discard that would otherwise be thrown away.
Prep Time: 15 mins
Cook Time: 35 mins
Additional Time: 1 hour 30 mins
Total Time: 2 hours 20 mins
Servings: 12
Ingredients
1 cup (240 grams) water, warmed to 100-110 degrees F
2 1/4 teaspoons (7 grams) active dry yeast
1/2 cup (140 grams) sourdough discard
2 tablespoons (42 grams) honey
2 tablespoons (28 grams) butter, at soft room temperature
3 cups (360 grams) all purpose flour
1 teaspoon (3 grams) kosher salt
Directions
In a large bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer, combine the warm water and yeast. Let sit for 5 minutes until foamy.
Add the sourdough discard, honey, and butter to the yeast mixture. Stir to combine.
Add the flour and salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough by hand for 8-10 minutes, or in a stand mixer with the dough hook for 5-6 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Punch down the dough. Shape into a loaf and place in a greased 9x5 inch loaf pan.
Cover and let rise until the dough crowns about 1 inch above the rim of the pan, about 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
Bake for 30-35 minutes until golden brown and the internal temperature reads 190-200 degrees F.
Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely before slicing.
Nutrition Facts
Calories 174  Total Carbohydrate 32g  Dietary Fiber 1g  Total Sugars 4g  Protein 5g  Total Fat 3g  Saturated Fat 1g  Cholesterol 5mg  Sodium 180mg
